nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 澳大利亚
nodes.summary: 澳大利亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联221种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等221种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 红尾黑凤头鹦鹉
nodes.summary: 红尾黑凤头鹦鹉（Red-tailed black cockatoo，学名 Calyptorhynchus banksii），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于澳大利亚、雨林，常见栖息地包括热带雨林、灌丛、稀树草原，主要威胁包括森林砍伐、非法贸易。

nodes.name: 雨林
nodes.summary: 雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联99种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、鳳冠孔雀雉、Amboyna cuckoo-dove等99种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 热带雨林
nodes.summary: 结构复杂且冠层完整的高生物多样性森林。

nodes.name: 非法贸易
nodes.summary: 为宠物或展示用途进行非法捕捉与交易。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: samueli | range_map = Red-tailed Black Cockatoo.JPG | range_map_caption = Red-tailed black cockatoo range (in red) }} thumb|Female, Northern Territory thumb|Male, Northern Territory The red-tailed black cockatoo (Calyptorhynchus banksii) also known as Banksian- or Banks' black cockatoo, is a large black cockatoo native to Australia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in a wide variety of habitats, from shrublands and grasslands through eucalypt, sheoak and Acacia woodlands, to dense tropical rainforests. The bird is dependent on large, old eucalypts for nesting hollows, although the specific gums used vary in different parts of the country

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Populations in southeastern Australia are threatened by deforestation and other habitat alterations

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: On the 2007 advisory list of threatened vertebrate fauna in Victoria, this subspecies is listed as endangered. *The red-tailed black cockatoo is listed as vulnerable on the NSW Threatened Species Conservation Act 1995 Like many Australian cockatoos and parrots, the red-tailed black cockatoo is threatened by the thriving illegal trade in bird smuggling. High demand and high transit mortality mean that many more birds are taken from the wild than actually sold. In 1997, the Northern Territory Government's Department of Natural Resources, Environment and The Arts (now defunct) proposed a plan for management of the trade in eggs and nestlings of C. b
